rows.finalText: For "What is the next right step in my calling", the spread keeps returning to purpose / calling themes with a right path emphasis. The sequence begins in Past with Whip (11), so notice patterns that keep replaying is already in play. Here, Whip emphasizes repetition, friction, discipline, and the strain of a pattern that keeps replaying. Present turns on Counterpart (28), bringing the other person's perspective to the hinge of the reading and asking for one careful adjustment. Counterpart often points to the other person's position, their motives. The likely direction shows in Future through Ship (3), and it suggests movement away from the familiar broadens perspective once the pivot is met directly. Ship often points to movement, distance, trade, and the shift that comes once something leaves its old context in your calling. Whip (11) with Counterpart (28) show what most shapes the question of calling: a blend of repetition and other, asking you to turn friction into intentional practice while staying alert to projecting assumptions onto others. Counterpart (28) shows where the whole sequence either opens or tightens. Counterpart colors your calling through the other person's position, their motives, and the part of the story you do not control directly. Whip (11) with Counterpart (28) keep emphasizing a blend of repetition and other, asking you to turn friction into intentional practice while staying alert to projecting assumptions onto others. Clarify roles and expectations so Ship (3) can land as lived experience rather than just possibility.
